summary: Package fetches and executes payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: dist/bootstrap.js references the URL https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev together with two fetch() calls and reads from process.env. The destination is an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket — a known payload-distribution infrastructure pattern used to ship mutable binaries with no version pinning, no hash verification, and no relationship to a declared publisher. The URL pattern (pub-<32-hex>.r2.dev) is the same anonymous-bucket shape previously observed in npm dropper clusters. Fetching executable content from an anonymous, mutable R2 bucket and processing environment variables in the same lifecycle path is not a legitimate distribution channel for an MCP/ANVISA bulário lookup tool, whose stated purpose involves querying a Brazilian drug-information service and would not require pulling binaries from anonymous infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
